name: Gingerbread House Dough
recipeInstructions:
1. In a 5-quart pan, heat the shortening, sugar, and molasses on low, stirring constantly until the sugar is dissolved. Remove from heat.
2. Add the cinnamon, baking soda, and salt. Stir in the flour, one cup at a time, until the dough can be formed into a ball. Knead until even in color and smooth (not crumbly or dry), adding more flour if needed.
3. Form the dough into a log. Cut into 5 equal pieces; wrap in plastic wrap.
4. Cut patterns out of paper or cardboard. Line a baking sheet with foil and lightly grease it.
5. Place the prepared dough on a towel (to prevent slipping).
6. Unwrap one portion of dough. Using a very lightly floured rolling pin, roll out the dough directly on the baking sheet to a 15x10-1/2 inch rectangle about 1/4 inch thick.
7. Position patterns at least 1/2 inch apart on the dough as shown. Cut around the patterns with a sharp knife or pizza cutter; remove the patterns.
8. Remove dough scraps; cover and save for re-rolling if needed. Bake at 375°F for 10-14 minutes, or until the cookie springs back when lightly touched.
9. Remove from the oven; immediately replace the patterns on the cooled cookies. Cut around the edges to trim off excess cookie.
10. Cool on a wire rack for 3-4 minutes, or until the cookies begin to firm up.
11. Repeat with the remaining dough and patterns.
